subject: Databases
title: Index Bloat
description: A quick, practical guide to Index Bloat.

hook.text: After millions of updates and deletes, an index can be twice its optimal size. That's index bloat.

buildup.text: Postgres marks deleted rows as dead but doesn't reclaim the space immediately. The index grows with dead entries.

discovery.text: VACUUM reclaims dead space in tables. REINDEX rebuilds the index from scratch, compacting it.

twist.text: REINDEX locks the table. In Postgres 12+, REINDEX CONCURRENTLY rebuilds without blocking reads or writes.

climax.text: Monitor index size vs table size. If the index is much larger than the data, bloat is likely.

punchline.text: Bloated indexes waste RAM and slow scans. VACUUM and REINDEX are your friends.

quiz.question: What causes index bloat in PostgreSQL?
options: Dead rows from updates and deletes aren't reclaimed immediately, wasting space
Too many columns in the index
The index is too small for the table
